title: Brownfield Requirements from Problem-Framing Handoff
prompt: Read `./access-review-modernization-problem-framing.md`. Work only from that file. Do not ask follow-up questions. If you need assumptions, label them clearly and continue. Produce a first-release requirements set that preserves the approved campaign-and-evidence-first coexistence direction, keeps the work in the requirements layer, preserves non-goals and unresolved questions, and prepares a clean downstream handoff to system-design.

assertions.name: stays-in-specification-phase
assertions.description: Output remains in the requirements layer and does not jump into architecture, migration sequencing, or implementation design

assertions.name: preserves-design-direction-boundary
assertions.description: Keeps the approved campaign-and-evidence-first coexistence direction explicit instead of collapsing it into generic modernization language

assertions.name: preserves-non-goals-and-open-questions
assertions.description: Carries forward release-1 non-goals and unresolved sync, tenant, and historical-data questions instead of silently deciding them

assertions.name: respects-no-invented-precision
assertions.description: Does not convert unsupported qualitative or unresolved bounds into hard quantitative requirements without labeling them as assumptions or open questions

assertions.name: prepares-downstream-handoff
assertions.description: Produces a structured requirements artifact shaped for downstream system-design and acceptance-criteria work
